title: ricinus communis subvar. purpurascens
intro: Ricinus communis subvar. purpurascens, also known as the purple castor bean, is a variant of the Ricinus communis plant, native to tropical Africa. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The plant's seeds are rich in ricinoleic acid, a compound with potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often extracted to create castor oil, which can be used topically or taken internally in small amounts.
